---
title: "Day 10: Final Project"
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
    center-title-slide: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: [default, ../../theme/dark.scss]
    logo: ../../theme/codercub.jpg
    highlight-style: dracula
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-gradient: "linear-gradient(135deg, #0a0e1a 0%, #0d2137 60%, #1a1f35 100%)"

---

## We've Covered A Lot!

- Week 1:
  - Learned about the different types and applications of AI
  - Working with data in Python using **pandas** and **matplotlib**
  - Supervised ML using **scikit-learn** to create regression and classifications models
  - Trained classification models for movement detection using the micro:bit

## We've Covered A Lot!

- Week 2:
  - Learned about neural networks
  - Used computer vision models: **Face detection**, **Object detection**, **Pose landmarker**, **Gesture recognition**, and **Image segmentation**
  - Experimented with prompting techniques using generative AI, including structure for music and 3D applications
  - Used image models to generate images and VLMs for analyzing images

# Wow, Great Job!

# Final Project

## What Will You Build?

- Don't want to start from a blank page? Here are some ideas to extend one of these existing notebooks...

## Option 1: Workout Tracker

- Start from: "Rep Counter Challenge!" (Day 6)
- Extend it: track two movements at once, add sounds, or create an overlay to display training data
- Stretch: log every rep to a list and chart reps-per-exercise with matplotlib

## Option 2: Rock, Paper, Scissors Tournament

- Start from: "Rock, Paper, Scissors" (Day 7)
- Extend it: add sound effects on a win, a "3...2...1...Show!" countdown, or best-of-3 rounds
- Stretch: two-player mode using both hands, and track the longest win streak

## Option 3: Gesture Art Studio

- Start from: "Gesture Painter" (Day 7)
- Extend it: assign colors to different gestures, add an eraser, or let two hands paint independently
- Stretch: send your finished painting into "Image-to-Image" (Day 9) and let AI remix it

## Option 4: Smart Gate Logger

- Start from: "Parking Gate Challenge" (Day 9)
- Extend it: support a list of allowed plates, or auto-close the gate a few seconds after opening
- Stretch: log every attempt (plate + allowed/denied) and chart entries vs. denials with matplotlib

## Option 5: Reactive 3D Avatar

- Start from: "3D Pose Avatar" (Day 6)
- Extend it: add bones between joints, change a sphere's color when an arm is raised, or add a squat counter
- Stretch: use "Mesh Generator" (Day 8) to have AI generate props or a scene around your avatar

## Option 6: AI Seeing Assistant

- Start from: "VLMs as Assistive Technology" (Day 9)
- Extend it: swap the free-text description for structured output (Pydantic) that returns specific fields (objects seen, hazards, colors)
- Stretch: log every detection to a dataframe and chart what the assistant "sees" most often

## Or Your Own Idea!

- You can also start completely from scratch, if you'd prefer
- Mix and match ideas across notebooks and even across weeks
- The micro:bits are also available, if you'd like to use CreateAI to train new actions

## Today's Class

- Choose your project from today's slides
- Extend an existing notebook (make sure you rename it) or create a new one and start from scratch
- Copy/paste code from other notebooks
- Ask for help if you need it!
- After ~75 minutes, "show and share" (optional)
